A.rationale: Reports patch() absent, cites get/post-only, names exact AttributeError, asks intent offering two real paths (add patch or use post).

B.rationale: Reports patch() absent, names exact AttributeError, proposes concrete real fix (add patch(self,path,body)). Tight, no needless hedging or wrong call.

C.rationale: Surfaces mismatch but actually wrote blind client.patch() call (the penalized behavior); quotes a docstring claim that may be fabricated. Longest.

notes: A and B both verify client.py and propose real paths without writing the broken call. C wrote client.patch() blindly and asserts an unverifiable docstring quote. A edges B by offering two options and confirming intent.
